_notes.source: IEEE C57.91-2011 Annex C (informative), Section C.2 — Calculation determining loading beyond nameplate rating of an existing transformer
_notes.rating: 186,666 kVA at ODAF cooling (third rating of a 112,000/149,333/186,666 kVA triple-rated unit)
_notes.insulation_class: 65°C rise, thermally upgraded kraft paper insulation
_notes.reference_hot_spot_temp_c: 110°C for normal aging rate, thermally upgraded insulation (IEEE C57.91 Section 7)
_notes.ambient_for_example: 30°C — Annex C Section C.2 uses Theta_A = 30°C throughout

name: ieee_annex_c_187mva_odaf
_notes_top_oil_rise: Delta-theta_or = 36.0 C: rated top-oil rise over ambient at full ODAF load. Stated on Annex C page 48.
_notes_hot_spot_rise: Delta-theta_HS,R = 28.6 C: rated hot-spot rise over top-oil at full load. Stated on Annex C page 48. Used in Equation C.4: Delta-theta_H = 28.6 * K^2.
_notes_thermal_time_constant: tau_o = 3.5 h: oil thermal time constant at rated load. Stated on Annex C page 48. Used in Equation C.2.
_notes_winding_time_constant: IEEE Annex C treats the winding hot-spot gradient as instantaneous for ODAF (no winding time constant in the worked example). We set tau_w = 0.05 h (3 min) so that for any dt >= 1 min, exp(-dt / tau_w) is negligible and the step is effectively instantaneous.
_notes_oil_exponent: n = 1.0 for ODAF. Annex C page 46 states explicitly: 'For the directed forced oil cooling, the n exponent is 1 and no correction of the time constant is required.' This makes tau_TO load-independent.
_notes_winding_exponent: Equation C.4: Delta-theta_H = Delta-theta_HS,R * K^(2m), with m = 1 for ODAF (IEEE Table 4). So winding_exponent y = 2m = 2.
_notes_load_loss_ratio: R = 4.87: ratio of load loss to no-load loss at rated current. Stated on Annex C page 48. Appears in Equation C.1: Delta-theta_TO,U = 36 * [(K^2 * 4.87 + 1) / 5.87].
_notes_hot_spot_factor: H = 1.0 (standard multiplier on the hot-spot gradient)
_notes_k_constants: k11 = 1.0: for ODAF (n = 1) no time-constant correction is needed, so k11 = 1. k21 = 1.0 and k22 = 1.0 because Annex C treats the winding hot-spot gradient as instantaneous — there is no two-component dynamic winding model in this example.
_notes_limits: Normal hot-spot limit 110°C per IEEE C57.91 Table 3 (thermally upgraded insulation). Top-oil limit 95°C per Table 3.
